root_cause: Control-plane availability is flapping, causing intermittent controller and scheduler progress
evidence: Control-plane state transitions observed: degraded -> recovered -> degraded -> recovered -> degraded -> recovered -> degraded
Components involved in flapping: kube-apiserver, kube-controller-manager, kube-scheduler
Control-plane flapping persisted for 19.0 minutes across 7 transitions
Deployment 'payments' intermittently stalls at 3/5 available replicas while only 3/5 replicas are updated
Kubernetes service 'kubernetes' still has ready endpoints, which points to intermittent component or control-plane path flapping rather than a steady API Service outage

causes.message: Timeline shows control-plane transitions: degraded -> recovered -> degraded -> recovered -> degraded -> recovered -> degraded
Control-plane components repeatedly alternate between degraded and recovered states
Controllers and scheduler intermittently lose API or lease progress during the flapping window
Deployment 'payments' intermittently stalls at 3/5 available replicas while only 3/5 replicas are updated

pod:payments-65b9c9d8d8-qzn9b: The pod belongs to a workload whose reconciliation is exposed to control-plane flapping
deployment:payments: Deployment 'payments' intermittently stalls at 3/5 available replicas while only 3/5 replicas are updated
control-plane:kube-apiserver: Readiness probe failed: Get "https://127.0.0.1:6443/readyz": context deadline exceeded
Readiness probe failed: Get "https://127.0.0.1:6443/readyz": context deadline exceeded
control-plane:kube-controller-manager: kube-controller-manager failed to renew lease kube-system/kube-controller-manager: Put "https://10.96.0.1:443/apis/coordination.k8s.io/v1/namespaces/kube-system/leases/kube-controller-manager": context deadline exceeded
kube-controller-manager successfully acquired lease kube-system/kube-controller-manager and became leader
control-plane:kube-scheduler: kube-scheduler leader election lost: failed to update lock kube-system/kube-scheduler: dial tcp 10.96.0.1:443: i/o timeout
kube-scheduler successfully acquired lease kube-system/kube-scheduler and became leader
service:kubernetes: Ready endpoints exist while control-plane components still alternate between failure and recovery

likely_causes: Intermittent API server or etcd latency is causing leader-election renewals and health checks to alternate between failure and recovery
Control-plane node network or host-resource pressure is briefly interrupting API access before recovering
Load balancer or Service VIP instability is intermittently breaking control-plane client paths
Control-plane static pods are repeatedly failing readiness probes and then recovering before a stable outage is established
suggested_checks: kubectl get pods -n kube-system -l tier=control-plane -o wide
kubectl get endpoints kubernetes -n default -o yaml
Compare kube-apiserver, kube-controller-manager, and kube-scheduler logs across the flapping window
